name: compas-timber

metadata.name: compas-timber
metadata.summary: COMPAS package for modeling, designing and fabricating timber assemblies.

documentation:
# compas_timber

[![Github Actions Build Status](https://github.com/gramaziokohler/compas_timber/workflows/build/badge.svg)](https://github.com/gramaziokohler/compas_timber/actions)
[![License](https://img.shields.io/github/license/gramaziokohler/compas_timber.svg)](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/compas_timber)
[![pip downloads](https://img.shields.io/pypi/dm/compas_timber)](https://pypi.python.org/project/compas_timber)
[![PyPI Package latest release](https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/compas_timber.svg)](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/compas_timber)
[![Supported implementations](https://img.shields.io/pypi/implementation/compas_timber.svg)](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/compas_timber)
[![DOI](https://zenodo.org/badge/DOI/10.5281/zenodo.7934267.svg)](https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.7934267)
[![Twitter Follow](https://img.shields.io/twitter/follow/compas_dev?style=social)](https://twitter.com/compas_dev)

![COMPAS Timber](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/gramaziokohler/compas_timber/main/docs/_images/compas_timber.jpg)

`compas_timber` is a user-friendly open-source software toolkit to streamline the design of timber frame structures. Despite its advances in digitalization compared to other building techniques, timber construction is often perceived as a challenging field, involving intricate processes in design, planning, coordination, and fabrication. We aim to increase the use of timber in architecture by lowering the threshold of creating versatile and resource-aware designs.

## Installation

> It is recomended you install `compas_timber` inside a virtual environment.

```bash
pip install compas_timber
```

To install `compas_timber` to Rhino run

```bash
python -m compas_rhino.install
```

## First Steps

* [Documentation](https://gramaziokohler.github.io/compas_timber/)
* [COMPAS TIMBER Grasshopper Tutorial](https://gramaziokohler.github.io/compas_timber/latest/tutorials.html)
* [COMPAS TIMBER API Reference](https://gramaziokohler.github.io/compas_timber/latest/api.html)

## Questions and feedback

We encourage the use of the [COMPAS framework forum](https://forum.compas-framework.org/)
for questions and discussions.

## Issue tracker

If you found an issue or have a suggestion for a dandy new feature, please file a new issue in our [issue tracker](https://github.com/gramaziokohler/compas_timber/issues).

## Contributing

We love contributions!

Check the [Contributor's Guide](https://github.com/gramaziokohler/compas_timber/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md)
for more details.

## Credits

`compas_timber` is currently developed by Gramazio Kohler Research. See the [list of authors](https://github.com/gramaziokohler/compas_timber/blob/main/AUTHORS.md) for a complete overview.

classes.name: compas_timber.connections.null_joint.NullJoint

methods.name: compas_timber.connections.null_joint.NullJoint.restore_beams_from_keys
documentation: After de-serialization, restores references to the main and cross beams saved in the model.

methods.name: compas_timber.connections.null_joint.NullJoint.add_features
documentation: This joint does not add any features to the beams.

class_variables.name: compas_timber.connections.null_joint.NullJoint.SUPPORTED_TOPOLOGY
documentation: TODO: this really supports all..

A null joint is a joint that does not have any features.

Can be used to join to beams which shouldn't join.

Please use `NullJoint.create()` to properly create an instance of this class and associate it with an model.

# Parameters

beam_a

: First beam to be joined.

beam_b

: Second beam to be joined.

# Attributes

beam_a

: First beam to be joined.

beam_b

: Second beam to be joined.

classes.name: compas_timber.connections.t_halflap.THalfLapJoint

methods.name: compas_timber.connections.t_halflap.THalfLapJoint.add_extensions
documentation:
Calculates and adds the necessary extensions to the beams.

This method is automatically called when joint is created by the call to `Joint.create()`.

Raises
------
BeamJoinningError
    If the extension could not be calculated.

Represents a T-Lap type joint which joins the end of a beam along the length of another beam,
trimming the main beam.

This joint type is compatible with beams in T topology.

Please use `THalfLapJoint.create()` to properly create an instance of this class and associate it with an model.

# Parameters

main_beam

: The main beam to be joined.

cross_beam

: The cross beam to be joined.

flip_lap_side

: If True, the lap is flipped to the other side of the beams.

cut_plane_bias

: Allows lap to be shifted deeper into one beam or the other. Value should be between 0 and 1.0 without completely cutting through either beam. Default is 0.5.

classes.name: compas_timber.connections.l_butt.LButtJoint

methods.name: compas_timber.connections.l_butt.LButtJoint.add_extensions
documentation:
Calculates and adds the necessary extensions to the beams.

This method is automatically called when joint is created by the call to `Joint.create()`.

Raises
------
BeamJoinningError
    If the extension could not be calculated.

methods.name: compas_timber.connections.l_butt.LButtJoint.add_features
documentation:
Adds the required extension and trimming features to both beams.

This method is automatically called when joint is created by the call to `Joint.create()`.

Represents an L-Butt type joint which joins two beam in their ends, trimming the main beam.

This joint type is compatible with beams in L topology.

Please use `LButtJoint.create()` to properly create an instance of this class and associate it with an model.

# Parameters

main_beam

: The main beam to be joined.

cross_beam

: The cross beam to be joined.

small_beam_butts

: If True, the beam with the smaller cross-section will be trimmed. Otherwise, the main beam will be trimmed.

modify_cross

: If True, the cross beam will be extended to the opposite face of the main beam and cut with the same plane.

reject_i

: If True, the joint will be rejected if the beams are not in I topology (i.e. main butts at crosses end).

# Attributes

main_beam

: The main beam to be joined.

cross_beam

: The cross beam to be joined.

small_beam_butts

: If True, the beam with the smaller cross-section will be trimmed. Otherwise, the main beam will be trimmed.

modify_cross

: If True, the cross beam will be extended to the opposite face of the main beam and cut with the same plane.

reject_i

: If True, the joint will be rejected if the beams are not in I topology (i.e. main butts at crosses end).

classes.name: compas_timber.connections.french_ridge_lap.FrenchRidgeLapJoint

methods.name: compas_timber.connections.french_ridge_lap.FrenchRidgeLapJoint.restore_beams_from_keys
documentation: After de-serialization, restores references to the top and bottom beams saved in the model.

methods.name: compas_timber.connections.french_ridge_lap.FrenchRidgeLapJoint.check_geometry
documentation: This method checks whether the parts are aligned as necessary to create French Ridge Lap and determines which face is used as reference face for machining.

Represents a French Ridge Lap type joint which joins two beam at their ends.

This joint type is compatible with beams in L topology.

Please use `LButtJoint.create()` to properly create an instance of this class and associate it with an model.

# Parameters

beam_a

: The top beam to be joined.

beam_b

: The bottom beam to be joined.

# Attributes

beams

: The beams joined by this joint.

joint_type

: A string representation of this joint's type.

reference_face_indices

: A dictionary containing the indices of the reference faces for both beams.

Calculates and adds the necessary extensions to the beams.

Raises

Represents a L-Lap type joint which joins the ends of two beams,
trimming the main beam.

This joint type is compatible with beams in L topology.

Please use `LHalfLapJoint.create()` to properly create an instance of this class and associate it with an model.

# Parameters

classes.name: compas_timber.connections.joint.BeamJoinningError
documentation:
Indicates that an error has occurred while trying to join two or more beams.

This error should indicate that an error has occurred while calculating the features which
should be applied by this joint.

# Attributes

beams

: The beams that were supposed to be joined.

debug_geometries

: A list of geometries that can be used to visualize the error.

debug_info

: A string containing debug information about the error.

joint

: The joint that was supposed to join the beams.

classes.name: compas_timber.connections.joint.Joint

methods.name: compas_timber.connections.joint.Joint.add_features
documentation:
Adds the features defined by this joint to affected beam(s).

# Raises

{class}`~compas_timber.connections.BeamJoinningError`

: Should be raised whenever the joint was not able to calculate the features to be applied to the beams.

methods.name: compas_timber.connections.joint.Joint.add_extensions
documentation:
Adds the extensions defined by this joint to affected beam(s).
This is optional and should only be implemented by joints that require it.

# Note

Extensions are added to all beams before the features are added.

# Raises

{class}`~compas_timber.connections.BeamJoinningError`

: Should be raised whenever the joint was not able to calculate the extensions to be applied to the beams.

methods.name: compas_timber.connections.joint.Joint.restore_beams_from_keys
documentation:
Restores the reference to the beams associate with this joint.

During serialization, {class}`compas_timber.parts.Beam` objects
are serialized by {class}`compas_timber.model`. To avoid circular references, Joint only stores the keys
of the respective beams.

This method is called by {class}`compas_timber.model` during de-serialization to restore the references.
Since the roles of the beams are joint specific (e.g. main/cross beam) this method should be implemented by
the concrete implementation.

# Examples

See {class}`compas_timber.connections.TButtJoint`.

methods.name: compas_timber.connections.joint.Joint.create
documentation:
Creates an instance of this joint and creates the new connection in `model`.

`beams` are expected to have been added to `model` before calling this method.

This code does not verify that the given beams are adjacent and/or lie in a topology which allows connecting
them. This is the responsibility of the calling code.

A `ValueError` is raised if `beams` contains less than two `Beam` objects.

# Parameters

model

: The model to which the beams and this joing belong.

beams

: A list containing two beams that whould be joined together

# Returns

{class}`compas_timber.connections.Joint`

: The instance of the created joint.

methods.name: compas_timber.connections.joint.Joint.ends
documentation: Returns a map of which end of each beam is joined by this joint.

methods.name: compas_timber.connections.joint.Joint.get_face_most_towards_beam
documentation:
Of all the faces of `beam_b`, returns the one whose normal most faces `beam_a`.

This is done by calculating the inner-product of `beam_a`'s centerline which each of the face normals of `beam_b`.
The face with the result closest to 1 is chosen.

# Parameters

beam_a

: The beam that attaches with one of its ends to `beam_b`.

beam_b

: The other beam.

ignore_ends

: If True, the faces at each end of `beam_b` are ignored.

# Returns

tuple(face_index, {class}`~compas.geometry.Frame`)

: Tuple containing the index of the chosen face and a frame at the center of if.

methods.name: compas_timber.connections.joint.Joint.get_face_most_ortho_to_beam
documentation:
Of all the faces of `beam_b`, returns the one whose normal is most orthogonal to `beam_a`.

This is done by calculating the inner-product of `beam_a`'s centerline which each of the face normals of `beam_b`.
The face with the result closest to 0 is chosen.

# Parameters

beam_a

: The beam that attaches with one of its ends to `beam_b`.

beam_b

: The other beam.

ignore_ends

: If True, the faces at each end of `beam_b` are ignored.

# Returns

tuple(face_index, {class}`~compas.geometry.Frame`)

: Tuple containing the index of the chosen face and a frame at the center of if.

methods.name: compas_timber.connections.joint.Joint._beam_side_incidence
documentation:
Returns a map of face indices of beam_b and the angle of their normal with beam_a's centerline.

This is used to find a cutting plane when joining the two beams.

# Parameters

beam_a

: The beam that attaches with one of its ends to the side of beam_b.

beam_b

: The other beam.

ignore_ends

: If True, only the first four faces of `beam_b` are considered. Otherwise all faces are considered.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> face_angles = Joint.beam_side_incidence(beam_a, beam_b)
>>> closest_face_index = min(face_angles, key=face_angles.get)
>>> cutting_plane = beam_b.faces[closest_face_index]
```

# Returns

dict(int, float)

: A map of face indices of beam_b and their respective angle with beam_a's centerline.

Base class for a joint connecting two beams.

This is a base class and should not be instantiated directly.
Use the `create()` class method of the respective implementation of `Joint` instead.

# Attributes

beams

: The beams joined by this joint.

ends

: A map of which end of each beam is joined by this joint.

frame

: The frame of the joint.

key

: A unique identifier for this joint.

features

: A list of features that were added to the beams by this joint.

attributes

: A dictionary of additional attributes for this joint.

methods.name: compas_timber.connections.l_miter.LMiterJoint.restore_beams_from_keys
documentation: After de-serialization, restores references to the main and cross beams saved in the model.

Represents an L-Miter type joint which joins two beam in their ends, trimming them with a plane
at the bisector angle between the beams' centerlines.

This joint type is compatible with beams in L topology.

Please use `LMiterJoint.create()` to properly create an instance of this class and associate it with an model.

# Parameters

beam_a

: First beam to be joined.

beam_b

: Second beam to be joined.

# Attributes

beam_a

: First beam to be joined.

beam_b

: Second beam to be joined.

functions.name: compas_timber.connections.solver.find_neighboring_beams
documentation:
Finds neighboring pairs of beams in the given list of beams, using R-tree search.

The inputs to the R-tree algorithm are the axis-aligned bounding boxes of the beams (beam.aabb), enlarged by the `inflate_by` amount.
The returned elements are sets containing pairs of Beam objects.

# Parameters

beams

: The list of beams in which neighboring beams should be identified.

inflate_by

: A value in design units by which the regarded bounding boxes should be inflated.

# Returns

list(set({class}`~compas_timber.part.Beam`, {class}`~compas_timber.part.Beam`))

# Notes

This is a `pluggable`. In order to use this function, a compatible `plugin` has to be available.
For example, in Rhino, the function {func}`~compas_timber.rhino.find_neighboring_beams` will be used.

classes.name: compas_timber.connections.solver.JointTopology

methods.name: compas_timber.connections.solver.JointTopology.get_name
documentation:
Returns the string representation of given topology value.

For use in logging.

Parameters
----------
value : int
    One of [JointTopology.TOPO_L, JointTopology.TOPO_T, JointTopology.TOPO_X, JointTopology.TOPO_UNKNOWN]

Returns
-------
str
    One of ["TOPO_L", "TOPO_T", "TOPO_X", "TOPO_UNKNOWN"]

Enumeration of the possible joint topologies.

Attributes
----------
TOPO_UNKNOWN
TOPO_I
TOPO_L
TOPO_T
TOPO_X

classes.name: compas_timber.connections.solver.ConnectionSolver

methods.name: compas_timber.connections.solver.ConnectionSolver.find_intersecting_pairs
documentation:
Finds pairs of intersecting beams in the given list of beams.

# Parameters

beams

: A list of beam objects.

rtree

: When set to True R-tree will be used to search for neighboring beams.

max_distance

: When `rtree` is True, an additional distance apart with which
  non-touching beams are still considered intersecting.

# Returns

list(set({class}`~compas_timber.parts.Beam`, {class}`~compas_timber.parts.Beam`))

: List containing sets or neightboring pairs beams.

methods.name: compas_timber.connections.solver.ConnectionSolver.find_topology
documentation:
If `beam_a` and `beam_b` intersect within the given `max_distance`, return the topology type of the intersection.

If the topology is role-sensitive, the method outputs the beams in a consistent specific order
(e.g. main beam first, cross beam second), otherwise, the beams are outputted in the same
order as they were inputted.

# Parameters

beam_a

: First beam from intersecting pair.

beam_b

: Second beam from intersecting pair.

tol

: General tolerance to use for mathematical computations.

max_distance

: Maximum distance, in desigen units, at which two beams are considered intersecting.

# Returns

tuple({class}`~compas_timber.connections.JointTopology`, {class}`~compas_timber.parts.Beam`, {class}`~compas_timber.parts.Beam`)
Provides tools for detecting beam intersections and joint topologies.

classes.name: compas_timber.connections.lap_joint.LapJoint

methods.name: compas_timber.connections.lap_joint.LapJoint.restore_beams_from_keys
documentation: After de-serialization, restores references to the main and cross beams saved in the model.

Abstract Lap type joint with functions common to L-Lap, T-Lap, and X-Lap Joints.

Do not instantiate directly. Please use `**LapJoint.create()` to properly create an instance of lap sub-class and associate it with an model.

# Parameters

main_beam

: The main beam to be joined.

cross_beam

: The cross beam to be joined.

flip_lap_side

: If True, the lap is flipped to the other side of the beams.

cut_plane_bias

: Allows lap to be shifted deeper into one beam or the other. Value should be between 0 and 1.0 without completely cutting through either beam. Default is 0.5.

# Attributes

main_beam

: The main beam to be joined.

cross_beam

: The cross beam to be joined.

flip_lap_side

: If True, the lap is flipped to the other side of the beams.

cut_plane_bias

: Allows lap to be shifted deeper into one beam or the other. Value should be between 0 and 1.0 without completely cutting through either beam. Default is 0.5.

functions.name: compas_timber.rhino.find_neighboring_beams
documentation:
Uses the Rhino.Geometry.RTree implementation of RTree to find neighboring beams.

# Parameters

beams

: The collection of beams to check.

inflate_by

: If set, inflate bounding boxes by this amount in all directions prior to adding to the RTree.

# Returns

list(set({class}`compas_timber.parts.Beam`))

: List containing sets or neightboring pairs beams.

methods.name: compas_timber.connections.butt_joint.ButtJoint.calc_params_birdsmouth
documentation:
Calculate the parameters for a birdsmouth joint.

# TODO: break this function into smaller more readable parts

Do not instantiate directly. Please use `**LapJoint.create()` to properly create an instance of lap sub-class and associate it with an model.

cross_beam

modify_cross

: If True, the cross beam will be extended to the opposite face of the main beam and cut with the same plane.

reject_i

: If True, the joint will be rejected if the beams are not in I topology (i.e. main butts at crosses end).

classes.name: compas_timber.planning.sequencer.Instruction
documentation: Base class for instructions

classes.name: compas_timber.planning.sequencer.Model3d
documentation: Instruction which incorporates a 3d model (beam, screw etc.)

classes.name: compas_timber.planning.sequencer.Text3d
documentation: Text overlay

classes.name: compas_timber.planning.sequencer.LinearDimension
documentation: 3d linear dimension

classes.name: compas_timber.planning.sequencer.Step
documentation:
Container for building instructions which assemble a single element

# Attributes

actor

: Actor which executes the step. one of [Actor.HUMAN, Actor.ROBOT]

element_ids

: List of cad element ids which are associated with the step.

elements_held

: List of cad element ids which are held by the actor (typically a robot) during this step.

location

: Location of the step.

instructions

: List of instructions which support the step.

is_built

: Whether the step has been executed.

is_planned

: Whether the step has been planned. Allows for incremental planning.

geometry

: Geometry type of the element to be assembled. One of ["obj", "cylinder", "box"]\]. Used for visualization.

priority

: Priority of the step. Steps within the same priority can be executed in parallel.

classes.name: compas_timber.planning.sequencer.BuildingPlanParser

methods.name: compas_timber.planning.sequencer.BuildingPlanParser.parse
documentation:
Parses building plan from json file.

# Parameters

filepath

: Path to json file

# Returns

{class}`BuildingPlan`

methods.name: compas_timber.planning.sequencer.BuildingPlanParser.serialize
documentation:
Writes building plan to json file.

# Parameters

building_plan

: Building plan to be serialized.

filepath

: Path to json file.

Provides class methods to parse and serialize building plans from and to json files.

This implementation does it the COMPAS way.
Implemet your own `parse()` and `serialize()` methods if you want to use a different format.

classes.name: compas_timber.planning.sequencer.BuildingPlan
documentation: Container for building steps, each steps is a collection of instructions which can be visualized

classes.name: compas_timber.planning.sequencer.SimpleSequenceGenerator
documentation:
Generates a simple sequence of steps, one step per element.
Order of steps is the same as order of elements in model.

# Parameters

model

: Model to be sequenced.

# Attributes

result

: Resulting building plan.

classes.name: compas_timber.design.workflow.CollectionDef

methods.name: compas_timber.design.workflow.JointRule.comply
documentation:

classes.name: compas_timber.design.workflow.DirectRule
documentation: Creates a Joint Rule that directly joins two beams.

classes.name: compas_timber.design.workflow.CategoryRule

methods.name: compas_timber.design.workflow.CategoryRule.reorder
documentation:
Returns the given beams in a sorted order.

The beams are sorted according to their category attribute, first the beams with `catergory_a` and second the
one with `category_b`.
This allows using the category to determine the role of the beams.

# Parameters

beams

: A tuple containing two beams to sort.

# Returns

tuple({class}`~compas_timber.parts.Beam`, {class}`~compas_timber.parts.Beam`)
Based on the category attribute attached to the beams, this rule assigns

classes.name: compas_timber.design.workflow.TopologyRule
documentation:
for a given connection topology type (L,T,X,I,K...), this rule assigns a joint type.

# parameters

topology_type

: The topology type to which the rule is applied.

joint_type

: The joint type to be applied to this topology.

kwargs

: The keyword arguments to be passed to the joint.

methods.name: compas_timber.design.workflow.JointDefinition.match

Container for a joint type and the beam that shall be joined.

This allows delaying the actual joining of the beams to a downstream component.

classes.name: compas_timber.design.workflow.FeatureDefinition

Container linking a feature for the beams on which it should be applied.

This allows delaying the actual applying of features to a downstream component.

classes.name: compas_timber.design.workflow.DebugInfomation

Container for debugging information allowing visual inspection of joint and features related errors.

joint_errors

{class}`~compas_timber.connections.BeamJoiningError`

classes.name: compas_timber.elements.wall.Wall
documentation:
Represents a single timber wall element.
Serves as container for beams joints and other related elements and groups them together to form a wall.

Wall is often a single unit of prefabricated timber wall element.
It is often refered to as an enveloping body.

TODO: complete this docstring

Create a timber assembly from a surface.

# Parameters

surface

: The surface to create the assembly from. must be planar.

beam_width

: The height of the beams aka thickness of wall cavity normal to the surface.

frame_depth

: The width of the beams.

stud_spacing

: The spacing between the studs.

z_axis

: Determines the orientation of the posts inside the frame.
  Default is `Vector.Zaxis`.

beams

: The beams of the assembly.

rules

: The rules for the assembly.

centerlines

: The centerlines of the beams.

normal

: The normal of the surface.

: The length of the panel.

panel_height

: The height of the panel.

frame

: The frame of the assembly.

jack_studs

: The jack studs of the assembly.

king_studs

: The king studs of the assembly.

edge_studs

: The edge studs of the assembly.

studs

: The studs of the assembly.

sills

: The sills of the assembly.

headers

: The headers of the assembly.

documentation: This is an internal method to generate process parameters

BTLxFrenchRidgeLap represents a fabrication process for creating a French Ridge Lap joint.

# Parameters

part

: The BTLxPart object representing the beam.

joint

: The joint object.

is_top

: Flag indicating if the part is the top part or bottom part.

# Attributes

PROCESS_TYPE

: The type of the process, which is "FrenchRidgeLap".

beam

: The beam object associated with the part.

other_beam

: The other beam object associated with the joint.

part

: The BTLxPart object this process is applied to.

joint

: The joint object.

orientation

: Indicates which end of the beam this join is applied to.

drill_hole_diameter

: The diameter of the drill hole.

ref_face_index

: The index of the reference face.

ref_face

: The reference surface frame object.

header_attributes

: The header attributes for the process.

process_parameters

: The process parameters that define the geometric parameters of the BTLx process.

angle

: The angle of the joint in degrees.

modules.name: compas_timber.solvers
documentation:
# solvers

```
.. currentmodule:: compas_timber.solvers
```

```
.. rst-class:: lead
```

This module contains pluggable functions for the sequence generating puzzle checker extensions.

## Pluggables

```
.. autosummary::
    :toctree: generated/
    :nosignatures:

    next_removable_part
    create_dissassembly_sequence
```

functions.name: compas_timber.solvers.next_removable_part
documentation:
Returns the next part to remove from the model.

One or more parts of the model are choosen to be removed next based on the particular solver implementation.

# Parameters

model

: The model to remove parts from.

removed_part_ids

: The list of part ids that have already been removed.

part_ids_to_remove

: The list of part ids that are to be removed.

number_of_parts_to_remove

: The number of parts to remove from the model.

# Returns

{class}`compas_monosashi.sequencer.Step`

: A step which contains one or more Instructions which specify
  the parts to remove from the model and the corresponding removal direction.

functions.name: compas_timber.solvers.create_dissassembly_sequence
documentation:
Returns a sequence of steps to disassemble the model.

This function's implementation decides on the strategy used to generate the collection of
steps to disassemble the model and determines their order.

The order of steps can be revered in order to generate a sequence of steps to assemble the model.

# Parameters

model

: The model to disassemble.

# Returns

{class}`compas_monosashi.sequencer.BuildingPlan`

: A sequence of steps to disassemble the model.

# See Also

{func}`~compas_timber.solvers.next_removable_part`

documentation:
Adds a blank extension to the beam.

start : float

documentation:
Returns the amount by which to extend the beam in each direction using metric units.

TODO: verify this is true
The extension is the minimum amount which allows all long faces of the beam to pass through
the given plane.

Returns
-------
tuple(float, float)
    Extension amount at start of beam, Extension amount at end of beam

methods.name: compas_timber.elements.beam.Beam.endpoint_closest_to_point
documentation:
Returns which endpoint of the centerline of the beam is closer to the given point.

# Parameters

point

: The point of interest.

# Returns

list(str, {class}`~compas.geometry.Point`)

: Two element list. First element is either 'start' or 'end' depending on the result.
  The second element is the actual endpoint of the beam's centerline which correspond to the result.

A class to represent timber beams (studs, slats, etc.) with rectangular cross-sections.

# Parameters

frame

: A local coordinate system of the beam:
  Origin is located at the starting point of the centerline.
  x-axis corresponds to the centerline (major axis), usually also the fibre direction in solid wood beams.
  y-axis corresponds to the width of the cross-section, usually the smaller dimension.
  z-axis corresponds to the height of the cross-section, usually the larger dimension.

length

: Length of the beam

width

: Width of the cross-section

height

: Height of the cross-section

# Attributes

frame

: The coordinate system (frame) of this beam.

length

: Length of the beam.

width

: Width of the cross-section

height

: Height of the cross-section

shape

: A feature-less box representing the parametric geometry of this beam.

blank

: A feature-less box representing the material stock geometry to produce this beam.

ref_frame

: Reference frame for machining processes according to BTLx standard.

ref_sides

: A tuple containing the 6 frames representing the sides of the beam according to BTLx standard.

ref_edges

: A tuple containing the 4 lines representing the long edges of the beam according to BTLx standard.

faces

: A list of frames representing the 6 faces of this beam.
  0: +y (side's frame normal is equal to the beam's Y positive direction)
  1: +z
  2: -y
  3: -z
  4: -x (side at the starting end)
  5: +x (side at the end of the beam)

centerline

: A line representing the centerline of this beam.

centerline_start

: The point at the start of the centerline of this beam.

centerline_end

: The point at the end of the centerline of this beam.

aabb

: An axis-aligned bounding box of this beam as a 6 valued tuple of (xmin, ymin, zmin, xmax, ymax, zmax).

long_edges

: A list containing the 4 lines along the long axis of this beam.

midpoint

: The point at the middle of the centerline of this beam.

key

: Once beam is added to a model, it will have this model-wide-unique integer key.

methods.name: compas_timber.fabrication.btlx.BTLxPart.ref_side_from_face
documentation:
Finds the one-based index of the reference side with normal that matches the normal of the given beam face.

This essentially translates between the beam face reference system to the BTLx side reference system.

# Parameters

beam_face

: The frame of a beam face from beam.faces.

: The key(index 1-6) of the reference surface.

Class representing a BTLx part. This acts as a wrapper for a Beam object.

# Parameters

beam

: The beam object.

# Attributes

attr

: The attributes of the BTLx part.

beam

: The beam object.

key

: The key of the beam object.

length

: The length of the beam.

width

: The width of the beam.

height

: The height of the beam.

frame

: The frame of the BTLxPart at the corner of the blank box that puts the blank geometry in positive coordinates.

blank

: The blank of the beam.

blank_frame

: The frame of the blank.

blank_length

: The blank length of the beam.

processings

: A list of the processings applied to the beam.

et_element

: The ET element of the BTLx part.

classes.name: compas_timber.fabrication.btlx.BTLxProcess
documentation:
Generic class for BTLx processings.

This should be instantiated and appended to BTLxPart.processings in a specific btlx_process class (eg BTLxJackCut)

each specific btlx process class should have:
PROCESS_TYPE a class attribute which matches the btlx process name
self.header_attributes which matches as a dict,
self.process_parameters which describe the geometric parameters of the process

the joint factory calls instantiates a process or processes and appends it or them to the BTLxPart.processes list

each process will have specific inputs which are derived from the Joint instance and related BTLxParts

some joints will require combinations of multiple BTLx processes, and some processes will cover multiple joint types.

the factory module should call the BTLx.register_joint(joint type, joint factory) function so that the BTLx class can call specific factory types.

The factory will typically derive the needed parameters from the Joint instance and the joint_factory will apply them to the individual BTLxParts.

# Parameters

name

: The name of the processing.

attr

: The attributes of the processing.

params

: The parameters of the processing.

# Attributes

et_element

: The ET element of the BTLx processing.

functions.name: compas_timber.utils.r_tree.find_neighboring_beams
documentation:
Uses RTree implementation from the CPython `rtree` library: <https://pypi.org/project/Rtree/>.

Returns a list of sets. Each set contains a pair of neighboring beams.
The beams are returned as sets as the order within each pair of beams doesn't matter.
That way there are no duplicates i.e. (beam_a, beam_b) == (beam_b, beam_a).

# Parameters

beams

: The collection of beams to check.

inflate_by

: If set, inflate bounding boxes by this amount in all directions prior to adding to the RTree.

# Returns

list(set({class}`~compas_timber.parts.Beam`, {class}`~compas_timber.parts.Beam`))

: List containing sets of two neightboring beams each.

documentation: TODO: calculate the topologies inside the model using the ConnectionSolver.

methods.name: compas_timber.model.model.TimberModel.process_joinery
documentation:
Process the joinery of the model. This methods instructs all joints to add their extensions and features.

The sequence is important here since the feature parameters must be calculated based on the extended blanks.
For this reason, the first iteration will only extend the beams, and the second iteration will add the features.

Represents a timber model containing different elements such as walls, beams and joints.

The timber model allows expressing the hierarchy and interactions between the different elements it contains.

# Attributes

beams

: A Generator object of all beams assigned to this model.

plates

: A Generator object of all plates assigned to this model.

joints

: A Generator object of all joints assigned to this model.

walls

: A Generator object of all walls assigned to this model.

center_of_mass

: The calculated center of mass of the model.

topologies

: A list of JointTopology for model. dict is: {"detected_topo": detected_topo, "beam_a_key": beam_a_key, "beam_b_key":beam_b_key}
  See {class}`~compas_timber.connections.JointTopology`.

volume

: The calculated total volume of the model.

functions.name: compas_timber.utils.compas_extra.intersection_line_line_3D
documentation:
Find, if exists, the intersection point of `line1` and `line2` and returns parametric information about it.

For each of the lines, the point of intersection and a `t` parameter are returned.

The `t` parameter is the normalized parametric value (0.0 -> 1.0) of the location of the intersection point
in relation to the line's starting point.
0.0 indicates intersaction near the starting point, 1.0 indicates intersection near the end.

If no intersection is detected within the max_distance, or the intersection falls outside either of the line segments,
[None, None], [None, None] is returned.

# Parameters

line1

: First line.

line2

: Second line.

max_distance

: Maximum distance between the lines to still consider as intersection.

limit_to_segments

: If True, the lines are considered intersection only if the intersection point falls whithin the given line segments for both lines.

tol

: The tolerance used for floating point operations.

# Returns

tuple({class}`~compas.geometry.Point`, float), tuple({class}`~compas.geometry.Point`, float)

functions.name: compas_timber.utils.compas_extra.intersection_line_plane
documentation:
Computes the intersection point of a line and a plane.

A tuple containing the intersection point and a `t` value are returned.

The `t` parameter is the normalized parametric value (0.0 -> 1.0) of the location of the intersection point
in relation to the line's starting point.
0.0 indicates intersaction near the starting point, 1.0 indicates intersection near the end.

If no intersection is found, [None, None] is returned.

# Parameters

line

: Two points defining the line.

plane

: The base point and normal defining the plane.

tol

: A tolerance for membership verification.

# Returns

tuple({class}`~compas.geometry.Point`, float)
